{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] {{Kibi clan}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == '''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == '''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|12=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|12=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|12=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|12=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|12=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|12=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|12=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|12=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|12=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|12=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|12=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|12=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|12=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|12=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|12=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]
