The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|12=draft|12=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|12=draft|12=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|12=draft|12=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft|12=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|12=draft|12=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

revision.comment: 20 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:稚武彦命 up to rev 87525202
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 稚武彦命 | 各国語表記 = わかたけひこのみこと | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = | 生地 = | 死亡日 = | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = [[播磨稲日大郎姫]]、伊那毘能若郎女 | 父親 = [[孝霊天皇]] | 母親 = [[絙某弟]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''稚武彦命'''（わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}）は、[[記紀]]に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「稚武彦命」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''若日子建吉備津日子命'''（わかひこたけきびつひこのみこと）」「若建吉備津日子命」、他文献では「若武彦命」とも表記される。 第7代[[孝霊天皇]][[皇子]]で、[[吉備氏|吉備臣（吉備氏）]]の遠祖。「吉備津彦命（きびつひこのみこと）」と称されることもあるが、その呼称では一般に兄の[[吉備津彦命|彦五十狭芹彦命（大吉備津彦命）]]を指す場合が多い。 == 系譜 ==
'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|倭国香媛}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|絙某弟}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[吉備津彦命|彦五十狭芹彦命
（吉備津彦命）]]|02='''稚武彦命'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』初出を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 第7代[[孝霊天皇]]と、[[絙某弟]]（はえいろど、蠅伊呂杼）との間に生まれた[[皇子]]である{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。同母兄には[[彦狭島命]]（日子寤間命）がいる。異母兄弟のうちでは、同じく吉備氏祖で[[四道将軍]]の1人の[[吉備津彦命|彦五十狭芹彦命]]（比古伊佐勢理毘古命、吉備津彦命）が知られる。 『[[日本書紀]]』では子に関する記載はない。『[[古事記]]』景行天皇段では、子として[[景行天皇]]皇后の[[播磨稲日大郎姫|針間之伊那毘能大郎女]]（日本書紀では播磨稲日大郎姫）、景行天皇妃の伊那毘能若郎女の名を記す。そのほか、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では[[吉備武彦|吉備武彦命]]を始めとする諸人物を子孫として記載する。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では、稚武彦命の事績に関する記載はない。 『[[古事記]]』孝霊天皇段では、若建吉備津日子命は大吉備津日子命（彦五十狭芹彦命）とともに[[吉備国]]平定に派遣されており、針間（播磨）の氷河之前（比定地未詳）に忌瓮（いわいべ）をすえ、針間を道の口として平定を果たしたという{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[日本書紀]]』では稚武彦命を吉備臣の祖とし、『[[古事記]]』では吉備下道臣・笠臣の祖とする{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 吉備朝臣 - 大日本根子彦太瓊天皇（孝霊天皇）子の稚武彦命の後。 * 左京皇別 下道朝臣 - 吉備朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 笠朝臣 - 孝霊天皇皇子の稚武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 笠臣 - 笠朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の鴨別命の後。 * 右京皇別 吉備臣 - 稚武彦命孫の御友別命の後。 * 右京皇別 真髪部 - 同命男の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 廬原公 - 笠朝臣同祖。稚武彦命の後。 === 国造 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[角鹿国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御代に吉備臣祖の若武彦命の孫の建功狭日命を国造に定める。のちの[[越前国]][[敦賀郡]]周辺にあたる『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 216。。 == 伝承 == 稚武彦命に関して、兄の[[吉備津彦命]]（大吉備津彦命）による吉備平定に付き従った際の伝承が各地に残っている。特に[[岡山県]][[総社市]]周辺では、吉備津彦命とともに鬼の[[温羅]]を討ったという伝説が知られる（詳しくは「[[温羅]]」を参照）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=稚武彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備氏]] {{デフォルトソート:わかたけひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:吉備氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|倭国香媛}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|絙某弟}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[吉備津彦命|彦五十狭芹彦命
（吉備津彦命）]]|02='''稚武彦命'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』初出を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 第7代[[孝霊天皇]]と、[[絙某弟]]（はえいろど、蠅伊呂杼）との間に生まれた[[皇子]]である{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。同母兄には[[彦狭島命]]（日子寤間命）がいる。異母兄弟のうちでは、同じく吉備氏祖で[[四道将軍]]の1人の[[吉備津彦命|彦五十狭芹彦命]]（比古伊佐勢理毘古命、吉備津彦命）が知られる。 『[[日本書紀]]』では子に関する記載はない。『[[古事記]]』景行天皇段では、子として[[景行天皇]]皇后の[[播磨稲日大郎姫|針間之伊那毘能大郎女]]（日本書紀では播磨稲日大郎姫）、景行天皇妃の伊那毘能若郎女の名を記す。そのほか、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では[[吉備武彦|吉備武彦命]]を始めとする諸人物を子孫として記載する。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では、稚武彦命の事績に関する記載はない。 『[[古事記]]』孝霊天皇段では、若建吉備津日子命は大吉備津日子命（彦五十狭芹彦命）とともに[[吉備国]]平定に派遣されており、針間（播磨）の氷河之前（比定地未詳）に忌瓮（いわいべ）をすえ、針間を道の口として平定を果たしたという{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔 == === 氏族 === 『[[日本書紀]]』では稚武彦命を吉備臣の祖とし、『[[古事記]]』では吉備下道臣・笠臣の祖とする{{Sfn|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 吉備朝臣 - 大日本根子彦太瓊天皇（孝霊天皇）子の稚武彦命の後。 * 左京皇別 下道朝臣 - 吉備朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 笠朝臣 - 孝霊天皇皇子の稚武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 笠臣 - 笠朝臣同祖。稚武彦命孫の鴨別命の後。 * 右京皇別 吉備臣 - 稚武彦命孫の御友別命の後。 * 右京皇別 真髪部 - 同命男の吉備武彦命の後。 * 右京皇別 廬原公 - 笠朝臣同祖。稚武彦命の後。 === 国造 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」には、次の[[国造]]が後裔として記載されている。 * [[角鹿国造]] - 志賀高穴穂朝（[[成務天皇]]）の御代に吉備臣祖の若武彦命の孫の建功狭日命を国造に定める。のちの[[越前国]][[敦賀郡]]周辺にあたる『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 216。。 == 伝承 == 稚武彦命に関して、兄の[[吉備津彦命]]（大吉備津彦命）による吉備平定に付き従った際の伝承が各地に残っている。特に[[岡山県]][[総社市]]周辺では、吉備津彦命とともに鬼の[[温羅]]を討ったという伝説が知られる（詳しくは「[[温羅]]」を参照）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=稚武彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|稚武彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備氏]] {{デフォルトソート:わかたけひこのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:吉備氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto}} {{Kibi clan}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{citation needed|Wakatakehiko}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == Genealogy ==

The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|彦狭島命|" to "|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = {{ill|Kōrei-tennō|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrei-tennō|qq=}} | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wakatakehiko]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01={{ill|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=}}|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a fellow ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}}. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]]

In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]]

Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[en:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Male Imperial Princes of Prehistoric Japan]] [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==

{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]].
