revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==

Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. In [[Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[ja:%E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11596350]] Wikidata name: Prince Wakatakehiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11596350]] Wikidata name: Prince Wakatakehiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==

{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tsuruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041477}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakatakehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ooiratsume|qid=Q11078587}}; {{ill|Kibinotakehikonomikoto|qid=Q18226257}}; {{ill|Q108044550|qid=Q108044550}}|father={{ill|Kōrei|qid=Q313041}}|mother={{ill|Q106617634|qid=Q106617634}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572}}; {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qid=Q65318}}; {{ill|Prince Hikosashima|qid=Q11487957}}; {{ill|Princess Chichihayahime|qid=Q10904825}}; {{ill|Princess Yamatototowakayahime|qid=Q11387099}}; {{ill|Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|qid=Q11387098}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11596350]] Wikidata name: Prince Wakatakehiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Wakatakehiko no Mikoto | native_name = わかたけひこのみこと | family_name = | image = | image_size = | caption = | title = | full_name = | Rank = | style = | personal_symbol = | birth_date = | birthplace = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = | issue = {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|1=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, Inabino Wakaratsume | father = [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei-tennō]] | mother = {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | positions = | religion = | signature = }} '''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto''' (稚武彦命, わかたけひこのみこと{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese royal figure recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and [[Kojiki]]. He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tsuruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041477}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakatakehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' further lists these clans as his descendants: * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kibi Asomi|ja|吉備朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto, son of Kōrei-tennō. * Left Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Shimotsumichi Asomi|ja|下道朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumichi Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto, grandson of Wakatakehiko. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Kasa Asomi|ja|笠朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa Asomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kasa Clan - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko’s son, Kamo Wakamiya no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: Kibi Clan - Descendants of Wakatakehiko’s grandson, Mitomo Wakamoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Magami-be|ja|真髪部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magami-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Descendants of Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto. * Right Capital, Imperial Line: {{ill|Iharahara Clan|ja|廬原公|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iharahara Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Related to Kasa Asomi. === Provincial Governors === The ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Predecessor Chronicles) lists the following provincial governors (''kokuzō'') among his descendants: * {{ill|Tsuruga Kokuzō|ja|角鹿国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tsuruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041477}}: During the reign of Emperor Keitai, a descendant of Wakatakehiko no Mikoto named Kenkosahi no Mikoto was appointed. This territory corresponds to modern-day [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]’s [[Tsuruga District]]''Studies on the Kokuzō System - Source Material and Analysis,'' Yagi Shoten, 2013, p. 216.. == Legend == Many regional traditions describe Wakatakehiko no Mikoto assisting [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]] in the pacification of [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. In [[:en:Soja|Soja]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], he is remembered for helping defeat the ogre {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}} alongside his brother (details can be found in the article {{ill|Ura (demon)|ja|温羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura (demon)|qq=|1=Ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17217710}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Wakatakehiko no Mikoto|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Lineages (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] {{Kibi clan}} {{translated page|ja|稚武彦命|version=87525202|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kibi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Wakatakehiko]] [[sn:E7%A8%9A%E6%AD%A6%E5%BD%A6%E5%91%BD]] [[sn:akatakehiko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakatakehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:稚武彦命]]

He was an [[Imperial Prince]] and ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he is referred to as "Wakatakehiko no Mikoto," while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' uses "'''Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto'''" (若日子建吉備津日子命), "Wakatake Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," and other names. He is sometimes associated with the name "Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto," though this generally refers to his elder brother, {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogical Diagram Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Wakoku Kahime}}|02={{Supra|7}}[[Kōrei-tennō|Emperor Kōrei]]|03={{color|#FC4E6B|Haeirodo}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!||||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto]]|02='''Wakatakehiko no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree/end}}
{{Smaller|(Names follow the first occurrence in the ''Nihon Shoki,'' with alternate names from the ''Kojiki'' and other sources in parentheses.)}} Wakatakehiko no Mikoto was the son of [[Kōrei-tennō]] and {{ill|Haeirodo|ja|絙某弟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haeirodo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (蠅伊呂杼){{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. His full brother was {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Hikokuguma no Mikoto). Among his half-siblings was {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}}, a fellow ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] and one of the [[Shidō shōgun]]. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not mention his children, but the ''[[Kojiki]]'' lists Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume (播磨稲日大郎姫) and Inabino Wakaratsume as his daughters. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' also names figures like {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|qq=}} as his descendants. == Historical Record == The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' does not provide details on Wakatakehiko no Mikoto's deeds. In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Emperor Kōrei’s account), Wakahiko Take Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|彦五十狭芹彦命|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Hiko Isasehiri no Mikoto (Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} to pacify [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]]. He established a ceremonial jar (Iwaibe) in Harima (present-day [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]), using it as a foothold to successfully complete the mission{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}. == Descendants == === Clans === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' identifies Wakatakehiko no Mikoto as the ancestor of the [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]]. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' also associates him with the {{ill|Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|ja|吉備下道臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Shimotsumichi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Kasa clan|ja|笠臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Wakatakehiko no Mikoto (Ancient Lineages)|2010}}.
